threat_model: GitHub check-run publication can affect repository branch-protection and readiness perception when a GitHub App has checks:write authority. The main risks are unapproved check-run writes, forged response evidence, token or private-key disclosure, tenant/repository spoofing, and overclaiming local proof as live GitHub mutation. The mitigation is to keep the planner non-live, hash the request payload, block secret-shaped material, require readiness evidence refs for all modes, forbid response evidence in plan-only and dry-run modes, and require operator approval plus external GitHub App execution receipt evidence before write-approved receipts admit an external write.

required_checks.check: plan-only and dry-run check proving the planner performs no GitHub API call and authenticates no request
receipt integrity check proving external execution evidence is required before write admission
audit visibility check proving repository, head SHA, and check-run output are bound into a deterministic request hash
redaction and secret absence check proving token-shaped metadata blocks receipt admission
policy bypass check proving non-write modes reject response evidence
IDOR tenant-scope check proving repository owner, repository name, tenant id, actor id, and command id are carried in the receipt boundary
protocol manifest check proving the public GitHub check-run write receipt schema is indexed

mitigations: require readiness evidence refs for all check-run write receipts
forbid response check-run evidence in plan-only and dry-run modes
require approval, installation id, external execution receipt, response id, and response payload hash for write-approved mode
block secret-shaped material before receipt admission
index the public receipt schema in the governance protocol manifest
avoid GitHub App token minting, branch protection mutation, or deployment claims
